took: One-question-at-a-time interview methodology; agent recommends an answer for each; prefer codebase exploration over asking when the answer is in the code.

Three-criteria rule for ADRs (hard-to-reverse + surprising + real trade-off); discipline of offering ADRs sparingly; CONTEXT.md compatibility shape; sharpening discipline (challenge fuzzy language, stress-test with scenarios, cross-reference with code).

Self-contained single-file HTML artifacts as design surfaces; no build step, no daemon assumption. The current skill adapts this for a server-backed living document, but the single-file ethos comes from here.

Inline-annotations-in-the-source-file pattern. Roughdraft uses CriticMarkup syntax embedded in markdown so the file itself carries comments, suggested edits, and metadata — the file is the canonical store, the viewer is just a renderer. We adapted this for HTML using semantic elements: <mark> for highlights, <aside data-comment-target> for paired comments, <article data-from> for chat turns. The HTML file IS the design + chat + decisions + annotations, with the server as a sync layer.
